activities_projects.title: Local Credit System Research Project
activities_projects.objective: To study and analyze different sources of credit available in the local area.
steps: Prepare a questionnaire about loan sources, interest rates, and repayment conditions.
Collect responses from at least 15 people (formal and informal borrowers).
Compare data using tables and analyze which source is safer and why.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand real-world credit systems and risks involved.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess survey quality, data accuracy, comparison, and analytical conclusions.

activities_projects.title: Impact of Interest Rates Study
activities_projects.objective: To analyze how different interest rates affect borrowers.
steps: Collect sample loan data with varying interest rates.
Calculate total repayment amounts.
Prepare a report explaining how high interest leads to debt traps.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically evaluate the burden of high interest rates.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check calculation accuracy and depth of explanation.

activities_projects.title: Formal vs Informal Sector Case Study
activities_projects.objective: To compare formal and informal credit systems through real examples.
steps: Interview one person who borrowed from a bank and one from a moneylender.
Compare documentation, interest rate, and repayment flexibility.
Prepare a comparative report with findings.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand practical differences and financial safety issues.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate research depth, clarity of comparison, and logical conclusions.

activities_projects.title: Digital Banking Awareness Campaign
activities_projects.objective: To promote safe digital banking practices.
steps: Research digital payment methods and common fraud risks.
Design awareness posters or pamphlets about safe usage.
Present the campaign message in class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand advantages and risks of digital transactions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess creativity, accuracy of information, and clarity of awareness message.

activities_projects.title: Debt Trap Analysis Project
activities_projects.objective: To examine causes and consequences of debt traps.
steps: Study a real or textbook case of a farmer or small trader in debt.
Identify causes of debt trap.
Suggest preventive measures and policy solutions.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand financial vulnerability and preventive strategies.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check analytical reasoning and practicality of suggestions.

activities_projects.title: Banking Services Field Study
activities_projects.objective: To analyze services offered by banks and their importance.
steps: Visit a bank (or research online) and list key services.
Categorize them into deposit, loan, and digital services.
Prepare a structured report explaining their role in development.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect banking services with economic growth.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess completeness of services listed and clarity of explanation.

activities_projects.title: Microfinance and Self-Help Groups Study
activities_projects.objective: To explore the role of microfinance in rural development.
steps: Research how Self-Help Groups (SHGs) provide credit.
Identify benefits for women and small entrepreneurs.
Present findings with examples.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand inclusive banking and rural credit systems.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate research accuracy and analytical depth.

activities_projects.title: Savings and Investment Planning Project
activities_projects.objective: To create a long-term financial plan.
steps: Select a future financial goal (e.g., higher education).
Calculate monthly savings needed with simple interest assumptions.
Prepare a financial plan with justification.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop financial planning and decision-making skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check planning accuracy and logical reasoning.

activities_projects.title: Evolution of Money Documentary
activities_projects.objective: To creatively present the evolution of money.
activities_projects.materials: Mobile phone camera, chart paper, editing app (optional)
steps: Research stages from barter to digital currency.
Create a short documentary or presentation.
Explain how each stage improved transactions.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand historical development of money systems.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess creativity, historical accuracy, and clarity of explanation.

activities_projects.title: Credit Policy Recommendation Report
activities_projects.objective: To suggest measures for improving access to formal credit.
steps: Study challenges faced by small borrowers in accessing bank loans.
Identify gaps in current credit system.
Prepare a recommendation report with practical solutions.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop policy thinking and analytical skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate depth of research, feasibility of recommendations, and clarity of report.
